blunt_diagnosis: The old loop is not a credible path to a Shor-level algorithm.
It searches tiny circuits and arbitrary oracle puzzles, then lets an LLM label complexity from N<=3 data.
Most successful runs rediscover Bernstein-Vazirani-like parity structure; most exotic names are unsolved or unsupported.
The dashboard's 'solved novel algorithms' criterion is just success_rate >= 1.0 on tiny instances, not a research result.
The project needs explicit scalable families, classical baselines, structural quantum handles, and kill criteria.

known_barriers: Strong Fourier sampling is insufficient for several symmetric-group cases.
Useful measurements may require entangled or collective measurements across registers.
domains.name: Nonabelian hidden subgroup and coset-state measurements
domains.why_high_upside: A general nonabelian HSP breakthrough could affect graph isomorphism, code equivalence, lattice automorphism variants, and group action problems.

known_barriers: Generic dihedral HSP has subexponential quantum algorithms but no known polynomial one.
Naive Fourier sampling reduces to hard subset-sum-like postprocessing.
domains.name: Hidden shift, dihedral HSP, and phase-state sieving
domains.why_high_upside: Hidden shift sits near the boundary between Shor-style Fourier methods and lattice-relevant subexponential algorithms.

known_barriers: Worst-case to average-case reductions can also imply resistance to simple algorithms.
Approximate periods can be too noisy for standard phase estimation.
domains.name: Lattice, number-field, and approximate periodicity problems
domains.why_high_upside: Factoring succeeds because periodicity survives noisy arithmetic structure. Finding analogous exploitable periodicity in lattices would be high impact.

known_barriers: Reduction to symmetric-group HSP inherits nonabelian Fourier-sampling barriers.
Random instances may hide too little exploitable representation structure.
domains.name: Code equivalence and algebraic isomorphism problems
domains.why_high_upside: A serious quantum improvement here would hit a major family of hidden permutation and isomorphism problems rather than a hand-built oracle.

known_barriers: Known negative results rule out simple strong Fourier sampling approaches.
Classical quasi-polynomial algorithms raised the bar for a meaningful speedup.
domains.name: Graph isomorphism beyond strong Fourier sampling
domains.why_high_upside: GI is not expected to be NP-complete and remains a central test case for nonabelian quantum algorithm design.

known_barriers: Many walks reproduce Grover-like quadratic gains only.
Spectral gaps and marked-state overlaps often vanish on hard instances.
domains.name: Quantum walks on algebraic and combinatorial state spaces
domains.why_high_upside: Quantum walks can turn geometry, expansion, and marked-subspace structure into query or time speedups without requiring full Fourier solvability.

known_barriers: Oracle separations often fail to transfer to explicit computational problems.
Artificial promise problems can look impressive while teaching little.
domains.name: Query complexity separations with recursive structure
domains.why_high_upside: New query separations can expose mechanisms later converted into algorithms, as happened historically with several oracle models.

known_barriers: Many candidates become incremental improvements to known simulation primitives.
Input models can hide the real cost in state preparation or data loading.
domains.name: Hamiltonian simulation, block-encoding, and tensor-network discovery
domains.why_high_upside: Block-encodings and simulation primitives can convert spectral structure into algorithms for linear algebra, optimization, and dynamics.

mission: Increase the chance of discovering or clarifying a genuinely major quantum algorithmic idea by searching for scalable structural advantage, not tiny demos.

ranked_candidates.classical_barrier: Code equivalence has hard average-case regimes and is a canonical hidden permutation problem.

flags: known no-go barriers must be confronted explicitly

experiments.kill_criteria: Max pairwise overlap stays above 0.9 or rank saturates at a constant.
experiments.positive_signal: Rank and pairwise distinguishability grow with instance count using low-degree invariants.
experiments.protocol: Generate code families with known permutation automorphisms. For each hidden permutation, compute equality-relation fingerprints and rank, then scale length and field size.
experiments.title: Coset-state relation rank sweep

experiments.kill_criteria: Required bond dimension or sample count grows exponentially on the first three scales.
experiments.positive_signal: Bond dimension grows polynomially while success probability beats individual-register tests.
experiments.protocol: Represent k-register measurement candidates as tensor networks over irrep labels. Optimize distinguishability on generated code instances.
experiments.title: Tensor ansatz for collective measurements

first_failure_modes: Coset fingerprints collapse to near-identical relation data.
Useful measurements require exponential tensor bond dimension.
The structured automorphism promise excludes hard instances.
ranked_candidates.hypothesis: Code equivalence instances with structured automorphism groups may expose low-rank collective coset-state observables missed by strong Fourier sampling.
ranked_candidates.problem_family: Families of linear codes with controlled automorphism strata, moving from small finite fields to asymptotic length n.
ranked_candidates.proposed_quantum_mechanism: Prepare multiple coset states for hidden permutation subgroups, search over entangled measurements using representation labels and tensor-network ansatzes.
ranked_candidates.title: Collective coset measurements for code equivalence
ranked_candidates.why_not_toy: The family is explicit, scales with code length, and has a real classical algorithmic baseline rather than an arbitrary oracle.

ranked_candidates.classical_barrier: Generic hidden shift and dihedral HSP connect to lattice-relevant hard cases; classical correlation search is exponential without exploitable structure.

flags: known no-go barriers must be confronted explicitly

experiments.kill_criteria: Large autocorrelation aliases or a simple classical correlation recovers the shift.
experiments.positive_signal: Flat spectra with low autocorrelation aliases and no obvious classical correlation handle.
experiments.protocol: For each algebraic base function, measure Fourier flatness, autocorrelation aliases, and shift distinguishability over growing domains.
experiments.title: Hidden-shift Fourier flatness and alias test

experiments.kill_criteria: Scaling matches generic subset-sum sieving with no family-specific gain.
experiments.positive_signal: Sample count and merge depth fit a polynomial or clearly improved subexponential law.
experiments.protocol: Simulate phase-state frequency samples and search for merge rules that increase useful phase precision faster than generic Kuperberg-style sieves.
experiments.title: Phase-combination sieve simulation

first_failure_modes: Fourier support is either too sparse and classical, or too random and subset-sum hard.
The sieve only works because the family is classically easy.
Noise in approximate shifts destroys phase coherence.
ranked_candidates.hypothesis: Some explicit hidden-shift families have phase-state distributions that avoid generic subset-sum hardness and admit polynomial-time sieving.
ranked_candidates.problem_family: Cyclic and dihedral hidden shifts where the shifted object comes from bent, multiplicative-character, or low-degree algebraic functions.
ranked_candidates.proposed_quantum_mechanism: Fourier sampling creates phase states whose frequency support has algebraic structure. A custom sieve combines phases without losing signal.
ranked_candidates.title: Structured hidden-shift sieve beyond generic dihedral HSP
ranked_candidates.why_not_toy: The work tests entire algebraic families and asks whether a known barrier has structured exceptions.

ranked_candidates.classical_barrier: Relevant regimes should map to lattice problems with worst-case or average-case evidence.

experiments.kill_criteria: False periods dominate or the ridge requires exponential precision.
experiments.positive_signal: A stable period ridge remains visible under polynomial precision.
experiments.protocol: Construct maps with known planted periods and noise. Measure whether period-preserving collisions dominate all other collisions as dimension grows.
experiments.title: Approximate-period collision landscape

experiments.kill_criteria: State preparation or arithmetic cost dominates the proposed speedup.
experiments.positive_signal: Map evaluation cost is polynomial with realistic precision overhead.
experiments.protocol: Estimate reversible circuit and precision costs for candidate maps before claiming any quantum advantage.
experiments.title: Reversible arithmetic cost audit

first_failure_modes: Coherence requirements exceed the precision needed to define the map.
Classical lattice reduction exploits the same approximate periods.
The candidate map is not efficiently reversible.
ranked_candidates.hypothesis: There may be number-field or lattice maps with approximate periods stable enough for phase estimation but hidden enough to resist classical sampling.
ranked_candidates.problem_family: Explicit maps from lattice points or ideals to coarse invariants with tunable noise, period rank, and collision profile.
ranked_candidates.proposed_quantum_mechanism: Use approximate QFT and phase estimation to recover dual-period information from coherent evaluations of noisy periodic maps.
ranked_candidates.title: Approximate period finding for lattice and number-field maps
ranked_candidates.why_not_toy: The objective is a reduction-quality scalable family, not a hand-marked solution state.

ranked_candidates.classical_barrier: Meaningful wins must beat modern quasi-polynomial classical GI baselines on well-defined families.

flags: classical baseline not hard enough yet
known no-go barriers must be confronted explicitly

experiments.kill_criteria: All gains reduce to known classical refinement invariants.
experiments.positive_signal: A candidate observable separates instances that strong Fourier labels cannot.
experiments.protocol: Reproduce failures of strong Fourier sampling on small GI-HSP instances, then mutate observables and record exactly which barrier is bypassed, if any.
experiments.title: Map the no-go boundary

first_failure_modes: The observable is equivalent to classical color refinement.
The family is already easy for modern classical GI.
Collective measurement construction scales exponentially.
ranked_candidates.hypothesis: Graph families with controlled refinement structure may admit collective observables that distinguish isomorphism cosets without solving full HSP.
ranked_candidates.problem_family: Strongly regular graphs, CFI-like constructions, and refinement-hard families with known automorphism behavior.
ranked_candidates.proposed_quantum_mechanism: Prepare graph-indexed coset or coherent refinement states and search for collective observables using tensor-network contractions.
ranked_candidates.title: Graph-isomorphism observables beyond strong Fourier sampling
ranked_candidates.why_not_toy: Graph isomorphism is a real structural problem with known quantum no-go zones.

ranked_candidates.classical_barrier: Choose distributions with known classical query lower bounds or reductions to hard property-testing tasks.

flags: known no-go barriers must be confronted explicitly

experiments.kill_criteria: Sparsity appears only after using exponentially many derivative settings.
experiments.positive_signal: Higher-order spectra become sparse while the original family remains classically hard.
experiments.protocol: For each nonlinear family, compute whether controlled finite differences produce sparse Fourier spectra over polynomially many derivative orders.
experiments.title: Derivative Fourier lift test

first_failure_modes: Derivative queries collapse the problem to classical low-degree learning.
The promise is artificial and has no explicit computational analogue.
The quantum routine is just BV on a relabeled oracle.
ranked_candidates.hypothesis: Nonlinear hidden structure may become tractable when expressed through higher-order Fourier or Gowers-uniformity observables rather than plain BV-style parity.
ranked_candidates.problem_family: Explicit low-degree phase polynomials, bent-function shifts, and locally testable hidden constraints over finite fields.
ranked_candidates.proposed_quantum_mechanism: Use phase queries and controlled derivatives to turn high-order structure into linear Fourier information over an expanded domain.
ranked_candidates.title: Higher-order Fourier search for nonlinear hidden structure
ranked_candidates.why_not_toy: Candidates must supply a scalable distribution and lower-bound target before any circuit search starts.

ranked_candidates.classical_barrier: Candidate invariants must not be cheaply approximable by classical randomized linear algebra on the same sparse access model.

flags: classical baseline not hard enough yet
known no-go barriers must be confronted explicitly

experiments.kill_criteria: Classical randomized estimators recover the invariant within the same asymptotic cost.
experiments.positive_signal: A separating invariant is quantum-estimable in polylog or polynomially lower cost.
experiments.protocol: Search for operator families whose spectra separate hard paired instances. For every hit, compare against classical trace/Lanczos estimators.
experiments.title: Invariant separation and access-model audit

first_failure_modes: The invariant is classically estimable by Lanczos or trace estimation.
Block-encoding construction costs more than the classical baseline.
The invariant fails to separate hard instances.
ranked_candidates.hypothesis: Quantum singular value transformation may expose algebraic invariants of graphs or codes that are expensive to estimate classically.
ranked_candidates.problem_family: Sparse matrices derived from graph lifts, code Tanner graphs, association schemes, and coherent constraint systems.
ranked_candidates.proposed_quantum_mechanism: Block-encode structured operators and use polynomial transformations or phase estimation to estimate invariants that separate hard instances.
ranked_candidates.title: Block-encoded spectral invariants for isomorphism-like problems
ranked_candidates.why_not_toy: The input model and block-encoding cost are part of the score, preventing hidden data-loading wins.

ranked_candidates.classical_barrier: Classical local search and MCMC mixing can be slow on these spaces; the target is a provable hitting-time separation.

flags: classical baseline not hard enough yet
known no-go barriers must be confronted explicitly

experiments.kill_criteria: Gap-overlap product predicts only quadratic or worse behavior.
experiments.positive_signal: Quantum hitting-time proxy improves asymptotically over classical and Grover baselines.
experiments.protocol: Build local-move graphs for each algebraic family. Track normalized spectral gap, marked overlap, and classical conductance proxies.
experiments.title: Spectral gap and marked-overlap sweep

first_failure_modes: The walk only recovers a quadratic Grover speedup.
The marked overlap vanishes faster than the gap helps.
Classical random walks mix just as well after better coordinates are chosen.
ranked_candidates.hypothesis: Some hard algebraic search problems have state-space graphs where the quantum walk spectral gap and marked overlap beat Grover-like behavior.
ranked_candidates.problem_family: Cayley and local-move graphs over code bases, lattice bases, isomorphism certificates, or partial algebraic assignments.
ranked_candidates.proposed_quantum_mechanism: Use coined or Szegedy walks to amplify structured marked subspaces whose geometry is invisible to unstructured search.
ranked_candidates.title: Quantum walks over algebraic solution complexes
ranked_candidates.why_not_toy: The experiment studies graph families, spectral gaps, and marked geometry over scaling instances.

coset_fingerprint_smoke_test.distinguishability: weak signal; likely needs a new observable

hidden_shift_random_baseline.shift_signal_quality: weak: likely classical correlation or noisy Fourier signal

cut_or_deprioritize: Arbitrary secret-bitstring oracle variants with no natural problem family.
N<=3 circuit searches used as evidence for asymptotic speedup.
State-preparation and unitary-synthesis tasks unless they directly support a larger algorithm.
LLM-only complexity claims without a lower bound, reduction, or scalable mechanism.
Dashboard labels that call tiny solved instances 'novel algorithms'.
revised_direction.one_sentence: Make this a research-program generator and structural-test harness for high-upside quantum algorithm mechanisms.
positive_filter: Quantum mechanism tied to Fourier sampling, coset states, phase estimation, walks, block-encodings, or tensor measurements.
A structural experiment with a positive signal and a kill criterion.
A path from toy finite instances to asymptotic proof obligations.
